title: Crispy Greek Chickpeas Pilaf
description: A salty, silky, kid-friendly Mediterranean dish featuring crispy chickpeas, fluffy quinoa, feta, and sundried tomatoes, all cooked in a savory vegetableoil base.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add chickpeas, season with salt, pepper, oregano, and crushed red pepper. Cook, stirring, until chickpeas are golden and crispy, about 8 minutes. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil and diced onion. Sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add cooked quinoa and vegetable broth. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes, allowing liquid to reduce slightly.
Fold in chopped sun‑dried tomatoes and crumbled feta, stirring until the feta starts to melt.
Return crispy chickpeas to the skillet and stir to combine.
Let the pilaf simmer for an additional 3 minutes to meld flavors.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Transfer to serving plates and serve warm; garnish with fresh oregano or a squeeze of lemon if desired.
